notes: Provisional source registry for GitHub issue #177. Copy approved entries into case-local source registries only when the corresponding case or document is added.

sources.title: Discours par Maximilien Robespierre -- 17 Avril 1792-27 Juillet 1794
sources.citation: Robespierre, Maximilien. Discours par Maximilien Robespierre -- 17 Avril 1792-27 Juillet 1794. Project Gutenberg ebook 29887.
sources.rights_rationale: Project Gutenberg identifies the ebook as public domain for U.S. reuse. The underlying speeches are eighteenth-century French public texts.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use the Project Gutenberg French source text. Extract only selected Robespierre speeches after confirming anchors and preserving provenance header.
sources.translation_risk_notes: Annotate French source text. English glosses are analytical aids only; do not commit modern English translations without separate review.
sources.notes: Approved for #178 as the initial French Revolution source base.

sources.title: Saint-Just Convention/Public Safety report candidate
sources.citation: Saint-Just report candidate; exact public-domain source edition not yet selected.
sources.rights_rationale: The underlying text is historical, but this review did not pin an exact public-domain edition and URL suitable for repository acquisition.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Do not acquire raw text until a specific Gallica, Wikisource, or equivalent public-domain edition is selected and reviewed.
sources.translation_risk_notes: Would require French source-language annotation with English glosses.
sources.notes: Deferred for #178 unless exact edition review is completed.

sources.title: Through Terror to Triumph: Speeches and Pronouncements of David Lloyd George Since the Beginning of the War
sources.citation: Lloyd George, David. Through Terror to Triumph: Speeches and Pronouncements of the Right Hon. David Lloyd George, M.P., Since the Beginning of the War. 1915. Internet Archive.
sources.rights_rationale: The selected edition was published before 1929 and is treated as U.S. public domain under standard U.S. term rules, subject to exact-edition verification.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use Internet Archive scan/OCR as source. Select speeches by phase, correct OCR against page images, and record anchors before ingestion.

sources.title: The Great Crusade: Extracts from Speeches Delivered During the War
sources.citation: Lloyd George, David. The Great Crusade: Extracts from Speeches Delivered During the War. 1918. Internet Archive.
sources.rights_rationale: The selected edition was published before 1929 and is treated as U.S. public domain under standard U.S. term rules, subject to exact-edition verification.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use Internet Archive scan/OCR as source. Select speeches by phase, correct OCR against page images, and record anchors before ingestion.

sources.title: Jefferson rough draft of the Declaration of Independence
sources.citation: Jefferson, Thomas. Rough draft of the Declaration of Independence, 1776. Founders Online, National Archives.
sources.rights_rationale: The underlying eighteenth-century document is public domain; Founders Online/National Archives provides citable scholarly metadata.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use the existing Founders Online acquisition path. Preserve draft status and editorial notes in metadata; do not silently merge with final Declaration text.

sources.title: Washington's Newburgh Address, 15 March 1783
sources.citation: Washington, George. Address to the Officers of the Army, 15 March 1783. Founders Online, National Archives.
sources.rights_rationale: The underlying eighteenth-century document is public domain; Founders Online/National Archives provides citable scholarly metadata.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use Founders Online if browser/CAPTCHA access permits; otherwise record a citable public-domain fallback edition before acquisition.
sources.notes: Approved for #180 if not duplicative with existing 1783 material.

sources.title: First Inaugural Address
sources.archive: Library of Congress or public-domain presidential text collection
sources.citation: Lincoln, Abraham. First Inaugural Address, 4 March 1861. Preferred source: Library of Congress Abraham Lincoln Papers or a verified public-domain presidential text collection.
sources.rights_rationale: Lincoln's 1861 presidential text is public domain. Use a pinned transcription source before ingestion.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Pin exact Library of Congress or equivalent public-domain transcription URL, then add verification anchors before acquisition.
sources.notes: Approved for #180 after exact URL is pinned.

sources.title: Special Message to Congress, 4 July 1861
sources.archive: Library of Congress or public-domain presidential text collection
sources.citation: Lincoln, Abraham. Special Message to Congress, 4 July 1861. Preferred source: Library of Congress Abraham Lincoln Papers or a verified public-domain presidential text collection.
sources.rights_rationale: Lincoln's 1861 presidential message is public domain. Use a pinned transcription source before ingestion.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Pin exact Library of Congress or equivalent public-domain transcription URL, then add verification anchors before acquisition.
sources.notes: Approved for #180 after exact URL is pinned.

sources.title: Proclamation to the Army of Italy, 1796
sources.archive: Gallica or equivalent public-domain French edition
sources.citation: Napoleon Bonaparte, Proclamation to the Army of Italy, 1796; exact public-domain French source edition to be pinned.
sources.rights_rationale: The French historical text is public-domain by age, but this review did not pin an exact reusable source edition. Do not use modern English translations as corpus text.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Before acquisition, pin the exact Gallica or equivalent French edition URL and record OCR/transcription quality.
sources.translation_risk_notes: Annotate French source text with English glosses only.
sources.notes: Conditionally approved for #180 only after exact source edition is pinned.

sources.title: Farewell to the Old Guard
sources.citation: Napoleon Bonaparte, Farewell to the Old Guard, 1814; exact public-domain French source edition not selected.
sources.rights_rationale: The source is short and historical, but no exact reusable source edition is pinned and the document is not necessary for the current expansion window.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Do not acquire in #180 unless a separate decision reopens Napoleon phase-closure material.
sources.translation_risk_notes: Would require source-language French annotation and glosses.

sources.title: Imperial Japan reserve source set
sources.citation: Imperial Rescripts and related Japanese source texts; exact source editions and translation policy not selected.
sources.rights_rationale: High comparative value, but source-language originals, official translations, and storage policy require a dedicated review.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Do not acquire during the first expansion pass. Reopen only if exact Japanese source texts and translation policy are resolved.
sources.translation_risk_notes: Japanese source-language annotation would require specialist review; English translations are not automatically reusable.

sources.title: Nazi support-material candidate layer
sources.citation: Goebbels or Goering support material candidate layer; exact source editions and limits not selected.
sources.rights_rationale: Existing open decision on support-material limits remains unresolved; source and translation/storage policy require a separate decision.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Do not acquire in the pre-v1 expansion window unless the support-material boundary is separately resolved.
sources.translation_risk_notes: German source-language annotation and English gloss policy would be required.
